findings: The semantic owner layers include Core, Governance, Cognitive, Action, and Memory.
Orchestration coordinates semantic layers using public contracts.
Interface presents and invokes the system via truthful surfaces.
Infrastructure provides technical support and does not define Jeff semantics.
Assistant is a future higher-level interaction behavior positioned above the other layers.
inference: The architecture is designed to separate semantic ownership from coordination and technical infrastructure.
summary: Jeff utilizes a structural architecture consisting of nine top-level layers, which are categorized by their roles as semantic owners, coordinators, presentation surfaces, technical support, or higher-level interaction behavior.
title: list the layers
uncertainty: The provided evidence lists eight specific layers (Core, Governance, Cognitive, Action, Memory, Orchestration, Interface, Infrastructure, and Assistant) but does not explicitly name a ninth distinct layer despite stating there are nine top-level layers.
